rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/25 a fully amended accounting petition to be filed. None of the issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing 6/26/24 have been addressed. A third accounting was filed instead, when this second one had not yet been adjudicated. Whatever is in the third and final account must be incorporated into the fully amended version. The amended accounting must be filed in time to give 15 days' notice of the 3/5/25 hearing. If it cannot be filed in time, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request a further continuance. When the fully amended second and final account is filed, the third accounting will be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCELLA PIERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/25. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice was mailed to two of the heirs at addresses that differ from the addresses shown in the petition to be theirs. (3) It does not appear that the petitioner or, now, administrator has ever disclosed whose children the grandchildren are, as required by LRSF 14.18 C. and D. Who was or were the decedent's child or children who was or were the parent(s) of the grandchildren and when did they die? (4) The accounting, while a little silly, since there is reportedly only real property, does not have a schedule showing the current fair market value, as required by Probate Code Section 1063(a). Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Despite what Counsel declared in a "Response to Probate Notes" filed on 10/30/23, courtesy copies were not provided for this petition either.) NB: All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAMONA LOPEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/30/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 12/10/24 is incomplete. It appears that certain pages were not properly e-filed. (2) Letters issued to Doris Lopez and certified within 60 days must be filed. If she was not yet appointed as the personal representative of the estate of Roberto Lopez on 8/16/24, she had no authority to act on behalf of that estate. (3) There were payments out of the sale proceeds to Ric Lopez and Rafael Lopez. Please explain how they do not contradict Administrator's allegation in paragraph 16 of his petition. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/15/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. Please read the single, whole sentence that is item 4 of the I&A.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROGER HENRY PEDEUPE REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED 5/23/18, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee; Determining The Validity Of Trust Provisions; And F
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need clarification whether the following heirs had been given 30-day notice at the correct address: Eva. Michelle and Philip. A new proof of service by mail filed 1/6/25 shows 28-day service. 2. The Court will determine on the request of appointment of the successor trustee. 3. The Court will determine on the validity of the Gift Letter as an amendment to the trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
